channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: After talking tough during campaign, Trump appears to ease up on China at start of presidency
item.description: On the campaign trail last year, President Donald Trump talked tough about imposing tariffs as high as 60% on Chinese goods and threatened to renew the trade war with China that he launched during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang And Ken Moritsugu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: New results from a national exam find that America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: JFK's daughter says cousin RFK Jr is a 'predator,' 'willfully misinformed' and 'unqualified' to lead HHS
item.description: Caroline Kennedy sent a letter to lawmakers urging them not to confirm her cousin Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to be the next Secretary of Health and Human Services, calling him "a predator" and arguing he is "unqualified" both professionally and personally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives. The order capped the most chaotic day for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump offering federal workers buyouts with about 8 months' pay in effort to shrink government
item.description: The Trump administration says it is offering buyouts to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by next week — an unprecedented move to shrink the U.S. government at breakneck speed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt made her debut in the briefing room on Tuesday and promised to speak frequently with the news media and open up the briefing room to podcasters and social media influencers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kern County Board of Supervisors votes to separate voter registrar duties of Auditor-Controller-Clerk's office
item.description: The Kern County Board of Supervisors voted Tuesday to restructure how the county registrar of voters functions within the current office of Auditor-Controller-Clerk.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats to hold ‘emergency’ meeting on Trump’s spending freeze
item.description: House Democratic leaders have arranged an “emergency” meeting of the full caucus on Wednesday in response to President Trump’s decision to freeze a large chunk of federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Sean Duffy to lead Department of Transportation
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) to be the next Transportation secretary, putting him in place to lead a sprawling agency that oversees air travel, highways, pipelines and railroads.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Trump voters are skeptical of his opening moves to embrace fellow billionaires
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Enrique Lopez votes sporadically but bought into Donald Trump’s vows to fight for everyday workers, helping the Republican flip Arizona last year. Then the home construction contractor watched how the billionaire president opened his second administration.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, BILL BARROW and AMELIA THOMSON DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A US shutdown on foreign aid is hitting worldwide. Rubio adds more exemptions
item.description: U.S.-funded aid programs worldwide have begun firing staff and preparing to shut down their operations as an unprecedented Trump administration freeze on foreign assistance brings their work to a sudden halt.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Samya Kullab, Farai Mutsaka And Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: News for Trump: Denmark's last envoy to Greenland says U.K. may have first dibs
item.description: Denmark's last minister for Greenland says Britain once claimed right of first refusal over the territory — because of its proximity to Canada, which Britain once ruled.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer, MPR News

item.title: Trump huddles with House GOP at retreat in Florida
item.description: GOP leaders will try to move Trump’s legislative agenda, including an extension of 2017 tax cuts and border policy.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Graham: Senate needs to investigate Trump decision to pull Bolton’s security
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) says the Senate needs to investigate President Trump’s decision to end his former national security adviser John Bolton’s personal security protection.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he still wants to buy Greenland, suggests Canada could become a U.S. state
item.description: The newly inaugurated president held forth on multiple foreign policy issues on Saturday, from Greenland to Canada to the war between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more refugees to 'just clean out' Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday he'd like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting to "just clean out" the war-torn area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC
